_comment: Ground truth annotation — GENOMICS checklist for Forster et al. 2020

article.title: Phylogenetic network analysis of SARS-CoV-2 genomes
article.authors: Forster P, Forster L, Renfrew C, Forster M

study_type.notes: Phylogenetic network analysis of 160 SARS-CoV-2 genomes. Uses median-joining network and Steiner algorithm.

1.notes: Title: 'Phylogenetic network analysis of SARS-CoV-2 genomes'

2.notes: Background on mitochondrial DNA phylogenetics, network methods, application to coronavirus evolution

3.notes: Sequences taken from GISAID database, not collected by authors. No clinical specimen collection described

4.notes: 160 complete genomes used, from 253 available in GISAID as of March 4, 2020. Selection: most complete genomes

5.notes: No sequencing done by authors — used publicly deposited sequences from 82 contributing labs

6.notes: No library preparation — used pre-existing sequences from GISAID

7.notes: No quality control of raw sequencing data — used deposited consensus sequences

8.notes: Aligned to Wu et al. reference genome (29,903 nt), truncated to consensus range 56 to 29,797

9.notes: Network5011CS software package; median-joining network algorithm and Steiner tree algorithm; epsilon=0 and epsilon=10

10.notes: Median-joining network (Bandelt et al. 1999) and Steiner algorithm (Polzin & Daneshmand 2003), 288 most-parsimonious trees of length 229

11.notes: Parsimony analysis, recurrent mutations at np11083 noted; gap coding as single deletion events

12.notes: No patient clinical/epidemiological metadata; only geographic origin and collection dates

13.notes: No sequencing quality metrics (sequences from database, not generated by authors)

14.notes: 160 human + 1 bat virus, 101 distinct genomic sequence types; range 56-29797; bat 96.2% similarity

15.notes: Three central variants A, B, C described with specific mutations (T8782C, C28144T, G26144T); amino acid changes detailed

16.notes: Figure 1: phylogenetic network with nodes proportional to taxa, notches for mutations; A/B/C cluster nomenclature; geographic distribution

17.notes: Not applicable — no drug resistance analysis for SARS-CoV-2 phylogenetics

18.notes: Infection tracing (Brazil-Italy, Ontario-Guangdong, Mexico-Italy-Germany-Wuhan chains); founder effects vs immunological adaptation hypothesis

19.notes: GISAID database (gisaid.org); Network5011 software at fluxus-engineering.com; supplementary data available

20.notes: Network5011CS software package freely available as shareware; coronavirus network files downloadable

21.notes: No ethics statement — uses publicly available database sequences, no patient contact

22.notes: No competing interest declared; acknowledgments to GISAID contributors and labs
